serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Mala is approximately one pack (65g dry weight), which is typically consumed as a snack or light meal.

nutrient_source.title: Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Mala — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values for energy (55 kcal/100g), protein (10.7g/100g), fat (0.0g/100g), and carbohydrate (2.36g/100g) sourced directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for this product. These values are noted to likely reflect the seasoning/flavouring component only rather than the complete prepared noodle dish. Secondary nutrient fields (sodium, saturated fat, dietary fibre, sugar) are estimated from the closest analogous ultra-processed salted egg seasoning product in USDA Branded Food database (USDA FoodData Central, FDC ID: 2099345, 'Salted Egg Seasoning Powder') and cross-referenced with HPB general guidance for mala-flavoured snack products. Fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB source entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — wheat noodles, cooked (analogous estimate)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Mala. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on cooked wheat noodles (GI ~47–54, low category) from the University of Sydney GI Database. A GI of 52 falls within the low GI range (≤54). The carbohydrate content of this HPB record (2.36g/100g) appears to reflect the seasoning component only; the glycemic load per serving is calculated as (52 × 2.36 × 0.65) / 100 = 0.80, rounded to 0.7 for the seasoning portion. If the full noodle dish is considered, GL would be substantially higher (estimated 15–25 per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Very high estimated sodium (~900mg/100g) from combined salted egg yolk seasoning and mala spice mix containing soy sauce, salt, and flavour enhancers
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Mala, as the estimated sodium content of approximately 900mg/100g far exceeds the 600mg/100g threshold for high-sodium foods; consuming even one pack contributes approximately 585mg sodium from the seasoning alone, and the full prepared dish would deliver substantially more.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: HPB-recorded carbohydrate (2.36g/100g) likely reflects seasoning only; the full prepared noodle dish would contain 40–60g carbohydrate per 100g, significantly impacting postprandial blood glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Mala: while the HPB-recorded carbohydrate of 2.36g/100g appears low, this likely reflects the seasoning component only, and the complete noodle dish would have a substantially higher glycaemic impact; patients should verify the full product nutrition label and limit portion size accordingly.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Ultra-processed product with high estimated sodium, salted egg yolk (dietary cholesterol and saturated fat source), and mala seasoning containing pro-inflammatory ingredients
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Mala due to its ultra-processed nature, high estimated sodium (~900mg/100g), and salted egg yolk content which contributes dietary cholesterol and saturated fat, all of which are associated with elevated cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Mala spice mix contains chilli (nightshade) and Sichuan peppercorn which may trigger RA flares; high sodium worsens joint swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Mala, as the mala seasoning contains chilli (a nightshade ingredient with solanine) and Sichuan peppercorn, both of which may aggravate RA flares in sensitive individuals, compounded by high sodium that worsens tissue and joint swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Mala is a Singaporean ultra-processed packaged noodle product combining two popular flavour profiles: salted egg yolk seasoning and mala (numbing-spicy) spice mix. The product is produced by Irvin's, a Singapore-based brand known for its salted egg snack range. The noodles are wheat-based and are typically prepared by tossing with the enclosed seasoning sachets. The flavour profile is intensely savoury, spicy, and numbing, with the characteristic richness of salted egg yolk balanced against the heat of chilli and the tingling sensation of Sichuan peppercorn. The mala seasoning typically contains chilli, Sichuan peppercorn, ginger, garlic, soy sauce, salt, and flavour enhancers. Nutritionally, the HPB Foodo database records energy at 55 kcal/100g, protein at 10.7g/100g, fat at 0.0g/100g (confirmed zero per HPB source, likely reflecting the dry seasoning component), and carbohydrate at 2.36g/100g. These values are understood to reflect the seasoning/flavouring component rather than the complete prepared noodle dish; the full dish would have substantially higher energy and carbohydrate content. Estimated sodium is approximately 900mg/100g, making this a high-sodium product unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The product is classified as ultra-processed and is not recommended for regular consumption by individuals with hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should verify the complete product nutrition label before consumption. The product is a popular snack and meal option in Singapore, often consumed at lunch or dinner.

confidence_reason: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate) are sourced directly from the HPB Foodo database with high confidence for the seasoning component. However, the HPB values are understood to reflect the seasoning/flavouring component only rather than the complete prepared noodle dish, introducing uncertainty about the nutritional profile of the full product as consumed. Secondary nutrients (sodium, saturated fat, dietary fibre, sugar) are estimated from analogous USDA Branded Food entries and HPB guidance, not from a direct product label measurement. GI is an analogous estimate. Overall confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. ZERO MACRO JUSTIFICATION (g4): fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database source entry for this product. This value likely reflects the dry seasoning component of the product, which is formulated without added fat in the seasoning powder fraction. The full prepared noodle dish would be expected to contain fat from the noodle base. saturated_fat_g is 0.0 consistent with fat_g being confirmed zero. 2. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES (g3): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are all set to -1: detailed micronutrient, fatty acid profile, and moisture data for this specific Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Mala product are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of April 2026. The HPB entry provides only the four core macros. 3. DATA SCOPE CAVEAT: The HPB-recorded per_100g values (energy 55 kcal, protein 10.7g, fat 0.0g, carbohydrate 2.36g) are atypical for a complete noodle dish and are understood to reflect the seasoning/flavouring sachet component only. The full prepared product would have substantially higher energy (~400–450 kcal/100g), carbohydrate (~55–65g/100g), and fat (~15–20g/100g). Users and clinicians should verify the complete product nutrition label. 4. SODIUM ESTIMATE: Sodium of 900mg/100g is an estimate based on analogous ultra-processed salted egg mala seasoning products in USDA Branded Food (FDC ID: 2099345) and HPB guidance; actual product sodium should be verified from the product packaging. 5. HALAL STATUS: Irvin's Salted Egg products are not universally certified halal; halal status should be verified from current product packaging. 6. GI NOTE: GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on cooked wheat noodles (low GI category, GI ≤54) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this product. gi_category is 'low' consistent with GI value of 52 (low range: ≤54).
